colony_mechanisms.M1_action_gate: PASS — tool diff in orient_sections.py (section_concurrent_activity + section_suggested_action)
colony_mechanisms.M2_external_injection: PARTIAL — references multi-agent coordination theory (concurrent cell tissue analogy from L-1184)
colony_mechanisms.M3_reward_targeting: reward channel: diagnosis-to-action bridge. Orient diagnoses concurrency; now prescribes mode.

baseline_targets.session_uniformity: 92% → sessions now differentiated by concurrency mode recommendation
baseline_targets.reward_alignment: diagnosis-to-action bridge: orient concurrency section now prescribes, not just reports

expect: Add concurrency detection to orient.py: detect N>=3 sessions via git reflog/working-tree analysis. Recommend verification/historian mode. Colony session 9/10.
actual: Enhanced section_concurrent_activity with 4-level classification (LOW/MODERATE/HIGH/EXTREME) based on active session count. N>=3: recommends verification/historian mode + git log re-check before each task. N>=5: warns about commit-by-proxy pattern. Enhanced section_suggested_action with concurrency-aware preamble. Uses existing claim.py heartbeat mechanism (no new detection method needed).
diff: Expected git reflog analysis. Used existing claim.py heartbeats instead — simpler, already reliable, no new mechanism needed. The detection already existed (claim.py sessions); the gap was recommendations, not detection. Key finding: diagnosis without prescription is the real concurrency problem — orient reported N sessions but never said what to DO about it.
external_ref: Multi-agent coordination: Woolley et al. (2010) collective intelligence factor — group performance depends on social sensitivity (reading other agents) not individual capability
